name: SecLists - installation (apt)
purpose: SecLists is the main collection of wordlists (discovery, passwords, fuzzing, payload lists). On Kali it installs as a single package, after which all the /usr/share/seclists/... paths from this section will work.
whenToUse: The first thing to install on a fresh system before any enumeration.

name: SecLists - git clone (always fresh)
purpose: Cloning the repository gives the freshest version of the wordlists (newer than the package in the distro repos) and works on any system, not only Kali.
whenToUse: When you need an up-to-date SecLists on a non-Kali system or newer than the packaged one.

name: rockyou - unpack
purpose: On modern Kali, rockyou.txt ships compressed (.gz) from the wordlists package. Until you unpack it, tools will not see it.
whenToUse: Right after installation: unpack it to get a ready /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt.

name: Assetnote wordlists - where to get them
purpose: Assetnote keeps auto-generated "fresh" wordlists (directories, API routes, subdomains) on its CDN - they reflect the real modern web and are often stronger than the classic SecLists.
whenToUse: When classic DirBuster/SecLists are outdated and you need a wordlist based on current statistics; downloaded as individual files via wget.

name: Where the wordlists live on Kali
purpose: A cheat sheet of the standard paths: /usr/share/wordlists/ - the shared folder (rockyou + symlinks to dirb/dirbuster/seclists/fasttrack/wfuzz); /usr/share/seclists/ - all of SecLists; /usr/share/wordlists/dirb/ and /usr/share/wordlists/dirbuster/ - the classic dirb/DirBuster lists.
whenToUse: When you do not remember where a needed list lives and which path to pass to -w.

name: Generating your own wordlists (cewl / crunch / rules)
purpose: When ready-made lists are not enough: cewl scrapes words straight from the target's site; crunch generates a brute-force set by mask/charset; hashcat/john rules mutate an existing wordlist (Password -> P@ssw0rd!).
whenToUse: For a specific target: collect words from its site (cewl) or expand a wordlist with rules during cracking.
